title: Escrow Config
description: Describes whether and how a tool supports escrow-protected transactions. The spec defines the interface and dispute model. The actual escrow logic lives in smart contracts or trusted services that implement this interface. Rule from v3 doc: no proof standard defined = no escrow-protected listing.

supported.description: Whether this tool supports escrow-protected payments.

type.description: smart_contract: funds held on-chain, released by contract logic. optimistic: funds auto-release after timeout unless disputed. trusted_third_party: a named third party holds and releases funds. Platform custody is not supported until legal review — see governance.md.

contract.description: For smart_contract type — on-chain contract details. The registry must verify the audit before allowing a tool to list with type: smart_contract. Self-declared audit URLs are not accepted; the registry sets audit_verified after independent review.

address.description: On-chain contract address (checksummed for EVM chains).

audit_url.description: URL to the security audit report submitted to the registry for verification. Tool authors submit this; the registry sets audit_verified after reviewing authenticity.

audit_firm.description: Name of the auditing firm (e.g., 'Trail of Bits', 'OpenZeppelin'). Registry verifies this against the document at audit_url.

audit_verified.description: Set by the registry after confirming the audit_url points to a genuine audit by audit_firm for this contract address. Tool authors must not set this field — it is populated by the registry during the smart_contract escrow review. A tool with smart_contract type and audit_verified: false cannot accept live escrow-protected payments.

audit_verified_at.description: Timestamp when the registry verified the audit. Set by the registry alongside audit_verified.

proof_of_delivery.description: REQUIRED for any escrow-protected listing. Every paid listing must define proof of delivery before escrow is funded. No proof standard = no escrow-protected listing.

type.description: hash_match: tool returns a hash that must match the declared result hash. access_granted: verifiable access (API key issued, auth token works). sandbox_completed: tool ran in sandbox with observed outputs. api_responds: API endpoint responds correctly to a test call. output_delivered: structured output matches declared schema. timeout_auto_release: optimistic — funds release after timeout unless disputed.

standard.description: Human-readable description of what constitutes successful delivery for this specific tool. Required — vague standards are rejected.

timeout_seconds.description: Seconds after payment before funds auto-release (for timeout_auto_release or as the window for other types).

verification_endpoint.description: Endpoint to programmatically verify delivery. Must accept a POST with {escrow_id, result_hash} and return {verified: bool}.

dispute.description: Three-tier dispute resolution. Designed so Tier 1 and 2 resolve automatically without human intervention. Tier 3 (human arbitration) should be rare by design.

window_seconds.description: How long after delivery the buyer can open a dispute. Buyer misses this window = auto seller win.

bonds.description: Dispute bond amounts scaled by transaction size. Prevents frivolous disputes. Bond is refunded if dispute is upheld, forfeited if not.

tiers.description: Three tiers in order. A dispute starts at Tier 1 and escalates if not resolved.

triggers.description: Conditions that cause this tier outcome to fire automatically.

resolution_sla_seconds.description: Maximum time this tier takes to resolve.

arbitration_endpoint.description: For Tier 3 only: endpoint to submit the dispute case to human arbitration.

non_delivery.description: Full refund if tool does not respond within timeout.

revenue_split.description: Declared sub-tool cost pass-through. Agents read this to understand total cost ceiling before calling.

max_sub_tool_spend_usdc.description: Hard cap on sub-tool spending per call. Buyer will never be charged more than this regardless of actual sub-tool usage.
